2603.26078.title: When Identities Collapse: A Stress-Test Benchmark for Multi-Subject Personalization
2603.26078.paper_summary: Subject-driven text-to-image diffusion models have achieved remarkable success in preserving single identities, yet their ability to compose multiple interacting subjects remains largely unexplored and highly challenging. Existing evaluation protocols typically rely on global CLIP metrics, which are insensitive to local identity collapse and fail to capture the severity of multi-subject entanglement. In this paper, we identify a pervasive "Illusion of Scalability" in current models: while they excel at synthesizing 2-4 subjects in simple layouts, they suffer from catastrophic identity collapse when scaled to 6-10 subjects or tasked with complex physical interactions. To systematically expose this failure mode, we construct a rigorous stress-test benchmark comprising 75 prompts distributed across varying subject counts and interaction difficulties (Neutral, Occlusion, Interaction). Furthermore, we demonstrate that standard CLIP-based metrics are fundamentally flawed for this task, as they often assign high scores to semantically correct but identity-collapsed images (e.g., generating generic clones). To address this, we introduce the Subject Collapse Rate (SCR), a novel evaluation metric grounded in DINOv2's structural priors, which strictly penalizes local attention leakage and homogenization. Our extensive evaluation of state-of-the-art models (MOSAIC, XVerse, PSR) reveals a precipitous drop in identity fidelity as scene complexity grows, with SCR approaching 100% at 10 subjects. We trace this collapse to the semantic shortcuts inherent in global attention routing, underscoring the urgent need for explicit physical disentanglement in future generative architectures.
2603.26078.paper_summary_zh: 主導的文本到圖像擴散模型在保持單一身份方面取得了顯著成功，然而它們在組合多個互動主體的能力上仍然大多未被探索且極具挑戰性。現有的評估協議通常依賴於全球 CLIP 指標，這些指標對於局部身份崩潰不敏感，並且無法捕捉多主體糾纏的嚴重性。在本文中，我們識別出當前模型中普遍存在的「可擴展性幻覺」：雖然它們在簡單佈局中合成 2-4 個主體表現出色，但在擴展到 6-10 個主體或面對複雜物理互動時卻遭遇災難性的身份崩潰。為了系統性地揭示這一失敗模式，我們構建了一個嚴謹的壓力測試基準，包含 75 個提示，分佈在不同的主體數量和互動難度（中性、遮擋、互動）上。此外，我們展示了標準的基於 CLIP 的指標在這一任務中根本上存在缺陷，因為它們經常對語義上正確但身份崩潰的圖像（例如，生成通用克隆）賦予高分。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了主體崩潰率（SCR），這是一種基於 DINOv2 結構先驗的新評估指標，嚴格懲罰局部注意力泄漏和同質化。我們對最先進模型（MOSAIC、XVerse、PSR）的廣泛評估顯示，隨著場景複雜性的增加，身份保真度急劇下降，在 10 個主體時 SCR 接近 100%。我們將這一崩潰追溯到全球注意力路由中固有的語義捷徑，強調未來生成架構中迫切需要明確的物理解纏。
2603.26078.author: Zhihan Chen et.al.
2603.26078.authors: Zhihan Chen, Yuhuan Zhao, Yijie Zhu, Xinyu Yao
